transcript: I'm a medical assistant at Legacy Health and I'm excited to show you what a day in my life is like let's go I leave for work around 7 15 a.m. since I get to wear scrubs to work it makes getting dressed really easy I usually drive to work but some people take public transportation I became an MA shortly after high school the training program took only eight months and I love being able to help people and make a contribution The first thing I do each day is put my stuff away in the break room. Then I clock in and check the patient load and schedule for the day. I also check the faxes and in-basket and generally get ready to see patients. We have such pretty views from our windows. One of the best things about working for Legacy is the people I work with. They're the best. Then it's time to see my first patient. My job is to take all their vitals and get them settled into the exam room and ready for the doctor. First, I get their weight and take their temperature. Once we're in the room, I get their blood pressure and pulse and confirm information from their patient record. I chat with the patients and try to make it feel like they are a person and not a number. I probably see an average of 15 patients a day. Then I let the doctor know when the patient is ready for them and tell them the details. Time for lunch. I usually eat in the break room with colleagues. If it's nice outside, sometimes I go for a quick walk. If a patient needs a shot, MAs can give the injection. We also do blood draws and prep instruments to be sent out and vials to go to the lab. In the afternoons, I check voicemail and call patients, mostly sharing test results and other information they need. You always want to make sure your patients have heard back from you. Then my shift is over and it's time to go home. I enjoy being an MA. It's not always easy, but it's a very rewarding career. That's my day. Hope to see you here.
